metadata.description: The friendly name for the workbook that is used in the Gallery or Saved List.  This name must be unique within a resource group.
The gallery that the workbook will been shown under. Supported values include workbook, tsg, etc. Usually, this is 'workbook'
The id of resource instance to which the workbook will be associated
The unique guid for this workbook instance

{"version":"Notebook/1.0","items":[{"type":1,"content":{"json":"# Entra - IAM Dashboard Dit dashboard geeft integraal inzicht in de belangrijkste IAM-signalen binnen Entra. De verschillende tabs combineren sign-in analyse, identity lifecycle-bewegingen, conditional access, externe samenwerking, privileged activity, workload identities en audit-events in één centraal overzicht. Gebruik de globale filters bovenaan om de gewenste periode en workspace te selecteren. Met de Help-schakelaar kan extra toelichting per tab worden getoond of verborgen. ### Overzicht van de tabs - **Overview** – algemeen overzicht van sign-in activiteit, trends, locaties, devices en foutanalyses. - **Joiner Mover Leaver** – inzicht in aangemaakte accounts, verwijderde/disabled accounts en wijzigingen in afdelingsgroepen. - **Conditional Access** – impactanalyse van conditional access policies voor gebruikers en workload identities. - **Guests & External** – zicht op guest accounts en cross-tenant inbound/outbound toegang. - **Privileged & Admin Activity** – monitoring van PIM-activaties en gebruik van high-privilege rollen. - **Workload Identities** – analyse van service principals en managed identities, inclusief failures. - **Audit Drilldown** – detailweergave van audit-events op basis van geselecteerde audit-operaties. Locations Location data color scheme is based on the amount of sign-ins. The gradient will display the darkest color for the most sign-ins. The data is aggregated from: - Location Details from each sign-in, which includes the country and city of the sign-in. - The status of sign-in: Success, Failure, or Interrupted. To view different types of sign-ins, select the corresponding tile at the top of this report to filter. #### Location Sign In Details This chart can be used to view details from each country in a list view. This workbook was designed to help admins understand the impact of their Conditional Access policies by aggregating sign-in results and highlighting users bypassing your Conditional Access policies. [Learn more](https://aka.ms/cainsights). ### Steps to deploy a new Conditional Access policy using this workbook 1. Read about [commonly applied Conditional Access policies](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/overview#commonly-applied-policies). 2. Create a new Conditional Access policy in [report-only mode](https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/concept-conditional-access-report-only). 3. In this report, select the policy from the Conditional Access policy parameter to see its impact summary. 4. Click on a result tile to filter the report by success, failure, user action required, or not applied sign-ins. 5. Verify whether the policy is acting as intended by looking at the breakdown per condition and sign-in status. 6. Edit your policy if necessary, or switch the state of the policy from report-only to on. 7. In this report, view the impact of all enabled policies by selecting "All enabled policies" from the Conditional Access policy parameter. op signin events van accounts van externe tenants ---"},"name":"text - 0"},{"type":1,"content":{"json":"### Discovery of cross-tenant access activity To use this workbook, you must first understand the difference between **outbound** access and **inbound** access. As businesses collaborate across organizations, they access external tenant resources or invite external users to access local resources using Azure AD B2B. Outbound access occurs when local users in your tenant access resources in external tenants. Inbound access occurs when external users access local resources in your tenant after signing in. With Azure AD interactive sign-in log telemetry, a tenant administrator can visualize and review the inbound and outbound cross-tenant activity. This workbook is divided into 4 steps, each of which builds on the previous step. You will start by selecting an external tenant represented by the tenantId in step 1. From there, you choose whether to focus on inbound access or outbound access in step 2. In step 3, you can select an app to zoom in on the users of that app in step 4. You can also disable showing this Showhelp with the toggle above. Additional information on using this workbook can be found at [Cross-tenant access activity
